label: Insightly
description: Arcade.dev tools for interacting with Insightly

tools.name: ConvertLead
tools.description:
Convert a qualified lead into a contact (when the lead names a person), an organization
(when the lead names one), and optionally an opportunity, then mark the lead converted.

The lead's name, email, phone, and title carry onto the contact, and its name/phone/website
onto the organization. A lead with only a last name becomes a contact under that name, since
Insightly stores a single-name person in the contact's first-name field. Insightly leaves the
lead's notes on the lead, so by default they are copied onto the new contact (or organization).

Fails if the lead has already been converted. Conversion is several creates followed by the
final lead update; it is not idempotent, so if a later step fails the lead stays unconverted
and any records already created remain. Check the lead in Insightly before retrying a failed
conversion so a retry does not duplicate records.

parameters.name: lead_id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the lead to convert.

parameters.name: create_opportunity
parameters.description: Also create an opportunity (true) or only a contact and org (false). Default false.

parameters.name: opportunity_name
parameters.description: Name for the created opportunity. Used only when create_opportunity is true.

parameters.name: pipeline_id
parameters.description: Pipeline id for the created opportunity. Used only when create_opportunity is true.

parameters.name: stage_id
parameters.description: Stage id for the created opportunity. Used only when create_opportunity is true.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: Monetary value to size the created opportunity. Used only when create_opportunity is true; omit to leave the value unset.

parameters.name: forecast_close_date
parameters.description: Forecast close date (YYYY-MM-DD) for the created opportunity. Used only when create_opportunity is true; empty to leave it unset.

parameters.name: responsible_user_id
parameters.description: User id to own the created opportunity. Used only when create_opportunity is true; omit to leave it unset.

parameters.name: copy_notes
parameters.description: Carry the lead's notes onto the new record (true) so the converted contact keeps the context that lived on the lead, or leave them on the lead only (false). Defaults to true.

tools.name: CreateNote
tools.description: Log a note against one CRM record. Provide exactly one record id to attach it to.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: The note text, such as a call summary or meeting recap.

parameters.name: title
parameters.description: Short subject line for the note. Empty derives it from the body's first line.

parameters.name: contact_id
parameters.description: Attach the note to this contact. Empty for none.

parameters.name: opportunity_id
parameters.description: Attach the note to this opportunity. Empty for none.

parameters.name: organization_id
parameters.description: Attach the note to this organization. Empty for none.

parameters.name: project_id
parameters.description: Attach the note to this project. Empty for none.

parameters.name: lead_id
parameters.description: Attach the note to this lead. Empty for none.

body.value: Had a 30-minute discovery call with the client. They expressed strong interest in the Enterprise plan and requested a detailed proposal by end of week. Key pain points discussed: lack of reporting visibility and manual onboarding process.

title.value: Discovery Call Summary – Enterprise Plan Interest

tools.name: GetContact
tools.description: Retrieve a single contact by its identifier.

parameters.name: contact_id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the contact.

tools.name: GetCurrentUser
tools.description:
Return the identity of the authenticated Insightly user (the "who am I" for this account).

Call this first to confirm who actions will be attributed to.

tools.name: GetLead
tools.description: Retrieve a single lead by its identifier.

parameters.name: lead_id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the lead.

tools.name: GetMyOpenWork
tools.description:
Roll up one user's open deals and open tasks in a single call.

Defaults to the caller (the authenticated user), so "what's on my plate" is one read instead
of a separate pipeline summary and task search. The summed open-deal value covers every
matched deal, not just the returned window.

parameters.name: responsible_user_id
parameters.description: Roll up the open work owned by this user id. 0 (the default) resolves the caller's own id, giving a 'what's on my plate' view.

parameters.name: opportunity_limit
parameters.description: Maximum open opportunities to include, soonest forecast close first (1-100). Defaults to 10.

parameters.name: task_limit
parameters.description: Maximum open tasks to include, soonest due first (1-100). Defaults to 10.

tools.name: GetOpportunity
tools.description: Retrieve a single opportunity by its identifier.

parameters.name: opportunity_id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the opportunity.

tools.name: GetOrganization
tools.description: Retrieve a single organization by its identifier.

parameters.name: organization_id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the organization.

tools.name: GetProject
tools.description: Retrieve a single project by its identifier.

parameters.name: project_id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the project.

tools.name: GetRecordActivity
tools.description:
Gather one record's recent notes and open tasks in one call, plus the deal stage.

Provide exactly one record id. Use this single call to prepare for a call or recap an
account instead of running separate note and task searches. When the record is an
opportunity, the response also carries that deal's current stage, state, and value; when
it is a project, it carries the project's parent opportunity and that deal's organization
so a delivery kickoff brief is one read; when it is a contact or organization, the response
also carries the opportunities and delivery projects that touch the account.

parameters.name: contact_id
parameters.description: Gather activity for this contact. Empty for none.

parameters.name: opportunity_id
parameters.description: Gather activity for this opportunity. Empty for none.

parameters.name: organization_id
parameters.description: Gather activity for this organization. Empty for none.

parameters.name: project_id
parameters.description: Gather activity for this project. Empty for none.

parameters.name: lead_id
parameters.description: Gather activity for this lead. Empty for none.

parameters.name: exclude_system_notes
parameters.description: Exclude Insightly's auto-generated stage/state/status-change notes so the brief shows only human-logged notes. Defaults to true.

parameters.name: note_limit
parameters.description: Maximum notes to include, most recent first (1-100). Defaults to 10. The response's note_count is the full match total; when it exceeds the notes returned, raise this to include more.

parameters.name: task_limit
parameters.description: Maximum open tasks to include, soonest due first (1-100). Defaults to 10. The response's open_task_count is the full match total; when it exceeds the tasks returned, raise this to include more.

parameters.name: linked_limit
parameters.description: Maximum linked opportunities and projects to include for a contact or organization, most recently updated first (1-100). Defaults to 10. The response's linked_opportunity_count / linked_project_count are the full match totals; when either exceeds the items returned, raise this to include more.

tools.name: GetTask
tools.description: Retrieve a single task by its identifier.

parameters.name: task_id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the task.

tools.name: ListLeadFieldOptions
tools.description:
List the account's configured lead statuses and lead sources so a natural-language
status or source name maps to the id that save_lead expects.

tools.name: ListPipelines
tools.description: List the configured pipelines, optionally filtered to opportunity or project pipelines.

parameters.name: for_opportunities
parameters.description: Filter to opportunity pipelines (true) or non-opportunity (false). Omit for all.

parameters.name: for_projects
parameters.description: Filter to project pipelines (true) or non-project (false). Omit for all.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum results to return (1-100). Defaults to 20.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: 0-indexed starting position. Defaults to 0.

tools.name: ListPipelineStages
tools.description: List a pipeline's stages in their defined order, so stage moves target real stages.

parameters.name: pipeline_id
parameters.description: The pipeline id whose stages to list.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum results to return (1-100). Defaults to 20.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: 0-indexed starting position. Defaults to 0.

tools.name: ListUsers
tools.description: List the users in the account, for attributing and assigning records.

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: Only return users whose email contains this text (case-insensitive). Empty matches all.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum users to return (1-100). Defaults to 20.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: 0-indexed starting position. Defaults to 0.

tools.name: SaveContact
tools.description: Create or update a contact. Omit contact_id to create (a first or last name is required).

parameters.name: contact_id
parameters.description: Omit to create a new contact; provide an existing id to update that contact.

parameters.name: first_name
parameters.description: First name. Sets the value when creating; changes it when updating, or leave omitted.

parameters.name: last_name
parameters.description: Last name. Sets the value when creating; changes it when updating, or leave omitted.

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: Email address. Provide to set or change it; empty string clears it; omit to leave it.

parameters.name: phone
parameters.description: Phone number. Provide to set or change it; empty string clears it; omit to leave it.

parameters.name: title
parameters.description: Job title. Omit to leave unchanged.

parameters.name: organization_id
parameters.description: Organization id to link the contact to. Omit to leave unchanged.

parameters.name: background
parameters.description: Freeform background notes. Omit to leave unchanged.

background.value: Met at SaaS Conference 2024. Interested in enterprise plan.

tools.name: SaveLead
tools.description:
Create or update a lead. Omit lead_id to create a new lead (supply a last name, an
organization name, or both).

parameters.name: lead_id
parameters.description: Omit to create a new lead; provide an existing id to update that lead.

parameters.name: last_name
parameters.description: Last name. When creating, supply this or an organization name (a company-only lead needs no person); when updating, omit to leave it unchanged.

parameters.name: first_name
parameters.description: First name. Omit to leave unchanged.

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: Email address. Provide to set or change it; empty string clears it; omit to leave it.

parameters.name: phone
parameters.description: Phone number. Provide to set or change it; empty string clears it; omit to leave it.

parameters.name: organization_name
parameters.description: The lead's organization name. Omit to leave unchanged.

parameters.name: title
parameters.description: Job title. Omit to leave unchanged.

parameters.name: lead_status_id
parameters.description: Lead status id. Omit to leave unchanged.

parameters.name: lead_source_id
parameters.description: Lead source id. Omit to leave unchanged.

tools.name: SaveOpportunity
tools.description:
Create or update an opportunity, including advancing its stage and recording won/lost.

Omit opportunity_id to create (a name is required). A new opportunity created without a
pipeline_id and stage_id is left unplaced: it still rolls up in the forecast under the
"(no stage)" bucket but cannot advance through a stage-based pipeline, so set pipeline_id
and stage_id together when you know the deal's pipeline and stage.

parameters.name: opportunity_id
parameters.description: Omit to create a new opportunity; provide an existing id to update it.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Opportunity name. Sets it when creating; changes it when updating, or leave omitted.

parameters.name: organization_id
parameters.description: Organization id to link the opportunity to. Omit to leave unchanged.

parameters.name: pipeline_id
parameters.description: Place the opportunity on this pipeline id. Combine with stage_id to also set the stage.

parameters.name: stage_id
parameters.description: Move the opportunity to this stage id (advance the deal). Omit to leave the stage as is.

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: Monetary value of the deal. Omit to leave unchanged.

parameters.name: state
parameters.description: Record this lifecycle state (for example mark won or lost). Omit to leave unchanged.

parameters.name: forecast_close_date
parameters.description: Forecast close date (YYYY-MM-DD). Omit to leave unchanged.

parameters.name: responsible_user_id
parameters.description: User id responsible for the opportunity. Omit to leave unchanged.

tools.name: SaveOrganization
tools.description: Create or update an organization. Omit organization_id to create (a name is required).

parameters.name: organization_id
parameters.description: Omit to create a new organization; provide an existing id to update it.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Organization name. Sets it when creating; changes it when updating, or leave omitted.

parameters.name: phone
parameters.description: Phone number. Provide to set or change it; empty string clears it; omit to leave it.

parameters.name: website
parameters.description: Website URL. Provide to set or change it; empty string clears it; omit to leave it.

parameters.name: background
parameters.description: Freeform background notes. Omit to leave unchanged.

background.value: Key enterprise client since 2018. Primary contact is the VP of Operations. Prefers communication via email.

tools.name: SaveProject
tools.description:
Create or update a delivery project, including advancing its stage.

Omit project_id to create (a name is required).

parameters.name: project_id
parameters.description: Omit to create a new project; provide an existing id to update it.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Project name. Sets it when creating; changes it when updating, or leave omitted.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Project status (for example mark completed). When creating, defaults to NOT_STARTED; when updating, omit to leave unchanged.

parameters.name: pipeline_id
parameters.description: Place the project on this pipeline id. Combine with stage_id to also set the stage.

parameters.name: stage_id
parameters.description: Move the project to this stage id (advance it). Omit to leave the stage as is.

parameters.name: opportunity_id
parameters.description: Opportunity id to link the project to. Omit to leave unchanged.

parameters.name: responsible_user_id
parameters.description: User id responsible for the project. Omit to leave unchanged.

tools.name: SaveTask
tools.description:
Create or update a task, optionally linked to a deal, project, contact, or org.

Omit task_id to create (a title is required).

parameters.name: task_id
parameters.description: Omit to create a new task; provide an existing id to update it.

parameters.name: title
parameters.description: Task title. Sets it when creating; changes it when updating, or leave omitted.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Task status; set it to COMPLETED to complete the task or a non-completed value to reopen it. Omit to leave unchanged.

parameters.name: due_date
parameters.description: When the task is due (YYYY-MM-DD). Empty string clears it; omit to leave it.

parameters.name: priority
parameters.description: Task priority. Omit to leave unchanged (defaults to NORMAL on create).

parameters.name: responsible_user_id
parameters.description: User id responsible for the task. Omit to leave unchanged.

parameters.name: project_id
parameters.description: Link the task to this project id. Omit to skip.

parameters.name: opportunity_id
parameters.description: Link the task to this opportunity id. Omit to skip.

parameters.name: contact_id
parameters.description: Link the task to this contact id. Omit to skip.

parameters.name: organization_id
parameters.description: Link the task to this organization id. Omit to skip.

title.value: Follow up with client about proposal

tools.name: SearchContacts
tools.description: Find contacts by name, email, or organization. Returns the account's default order.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: Match against contact name or email (case-insensitive substring). Empty matches all.

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: Only return contacts whose email contains this text. Empty for no filter.

parameters.name: organization_id
parameters.description: Only return contacts linked to this organization id. Empty for no filter.

parameters.name: updated_after
parameters.description: Contacts updated on/after this UTC date (YYYY-MM-DD). Empty = no lower bound.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum results to return (1-100). Defaults to 20.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: 0-indexed starting position. Defaults to 0.

tools.name: SearchLeads
tools.description:
Find leads by name, email, conversion state, or status. Returns the account's default
order.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: Match against lead name, organization, or email (substring). Empty matches all.

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: Only return leads whose email contains this text. Empty for no filter.

parameters.name: converted
parameters.description: true for converted leads only, false for unconverted only. Omit for both.

parameters.name: lead_status_id
parameters.description: Only return leads in this status (its id). 0 for no status filter.

parameters.name: updated_after
parameters.description: Leads updated on/after this UTC date (YYYY-MM-DD). Empty = no lower bound.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum results to return (1-100). Defaults to 20.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: 0-indexed starting position. Defaults to 0.

tools.name: SearchNotes
tools.description:
Read notes most recently created first within the scanned window, account-wide or for one
record.

Omit every record id for an account-wide activity feed (combine owner_user_id and the
created_after / created_before window for a standup-style recap), or provide exactly one
record id to read the notes on that single record. Date bounds are inclusive.

Notes are gathered by scanning the collection up to a ceiling and ordering that scanned set
most-recently-created first; when scan_truncated is true the scan stopped before reaching the
whole collection, so the newest notes may lie beyond the scanned window. Narrow with a record
id, owner, or a created_after / created_before window to bring the result within that window.

parameters.name: contact_id
parameters.description: Read notes attached to this contact. Empty for none.

parameters.name: opportunity_id
parameters.description: Read notes attached to this opportunity. Empty for none.

parameters.name: organization_id
parameters.description: Read notes attached to this organization. Empty for none.

parameters.name: project_id
parameters.description: Read notes attached to this project. Empty for none.

parameters.name: lead_id
parameters.description: Read notes attached to this lead. Empty for none.

parameters.name: record_types
parameters.description: Only return notes attached to a record of one of these kinds (for example to exclude auto-logged opportunity stage-change chatter from a recap). Omit for any kind.

parameters.name: exclude_system_notes
parameters.description: Exclude Insightly's auto-generated stage/state/status-change notes (across opportunities, projects, and tasks) so a recap shows only human-logged notes. Defaults to false (every note is returned).

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: Match this text against the note title or body (case-insensitive substring). Empty matches all notes in scope.

parameters.name: owner_user_id
parameters.description: Only return notes authored by this user id. 0 for no owner filter.

parameters.name: created_after
parameters.description: Only return notes created on or after this UTC date (YYYY-MM-DD). Empty for no lower bound.

parameters.name: created_before
parameters.description: Only return notes created on or before this UTC date (YYYY-MM-DD). Empty for no upper bound.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum results to return (1-100). Defaults to 20.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: 0-indexed starting position. Defaults to 0.

tools.name: SearchOpportunities
tools.description: Find opportunities by name, state, pipeline, or organization. Returns the default order.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: Match against the opportunity name (case-insensitive substring). Empty matches all.

parameters.name: state
parameters.description: Only return opportunities in this state. Omit for any state.

parameters.name: pipeline_id
parameters.description: Only return opportunities on this pipeline id. Empty for no filter.

parameters.name: organization_id
parameters.description: Only return opportunities linked to this organization id. Empty for no filter.

parameters.name: updated_after
parameters.description: Opportunities updated on/after this UTC date (YYYY-MM-DD). Empty = no lower bound.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum results to return (1-100). Defaults to 20.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: 0-indexed starting position. Defaults to 0.

tools.name: SearchOrganizations
tools.description: Find organizations by name or domain. Returns records in the account's default order.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: Match against the organization name (case-insensitive substring). Empty matches all.

parameters.name: domain
parameters.description: Only return orgs whose website contains this domain. Empty for no filter.

parameters.name: updated_after
parameters.description: Organizations updated on/after this UTC date (YYYY-MM-DD). Empty = no lower bound.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum results to return (1-100). Defaults to 20.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: 0-indexed starting position. Defaults to 0.

tools.name: SearchProjects
tools.description: Find projects by name, status, or pipeline. Returns the account's default order.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: Match against the project name (case-insensitive substring). Empty matches all.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Only return projects in this status. Omit for any status.

parameters.name: pipeline_id
parameters.description: Only return projects on this pipeline id. Empty for no filter.

parameters.name: updated_after
parameters.description: Projects updated on/after this UTC date (YYYY-MM-DD). Empty = no lower bound.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum results to return (1-100). Defaults to 20.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: 0-indexed starting position. Defaults to 0.

tools.name: SearchTasks
tools.description: Find tasks by title, status, completion, due date, owner, or link. Due bounds inclusive.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: Match this text against the task title (case-insensitive substring). Empty matches all.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Only return tasks in this status. Omit for any status.

parameters.name: completed
parameters.description: Filter by completion: false for open tasks, true for completed. Omit for both.

parameters.name: due_before
parameters.description: Only return tasks due on or before this date (YYYY-MM-DD). Empty for no upper bound.

parameters.name: due_after
parameters.description: Only return tasks due on or after this date (YYYY-MM-DD). Empty for no lower bound.

parameters.name: responsible_user_id
parameters.description: Only return tasks assigned to this user id. 0 for no owner filter.

parameters.name: project_id
parameters.description: Only return tasks linked to this project id. Empty for no project filter.

parameters.name: opportunity_id
parameters.description: Only return tasks linked to this opportunity id. Empty for no opportunity filter.

parameters.name: contact_id
parameters.description: Only return tasks linked to this contact id. Empty for no contact filter.

parameters.name: organization_id
parameters.description: Only return tasks linked to this organization id. Empty for no organization filter.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum results to return (1-100). Defaults to 20.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: 0-indexed starting position. Defaults to 0.

tools.name: SummarizePipeline
tools.description:
Roll up opportunities by pipeline stage with a count and summed value per stage.

A forecast view for closing out the week: groups are ordered by the stage's defined order.

parameters.name: state
parameters.description: Roll up only opportunities in this state. Defaults to OPEN (the live forecast); pass null to include every state.

parameters.name: pipeline_id
parameters.description: Roll up only opportunities on this pipeline id. Empty for all pipelines.

parameters.name: organization_id
parameters.description: Roll up only opportunities linked to this organization id. Empty for no filter.

parameters.name: responsible_user_id
parameters.description: Roll up only opportunities owned by this user id (the caller's own id gives a 'my pipeline' view). 0 for every owner.

parameters.name: include_empty_stages
parameters.description: Also list configured stages that hold no opportunities in scope, with a count and value of 0, so the forecast covers every stage rather than only the populated ones. Scoped to pipeline_id when set, otherwise every pipeline's stages. Defaults to false.

summary:
Arcade toolkit for [Insightly](https://www.insightly.com/), a CRM platform. Enables reading, creating, updating, and orchestrating CRM records — leads, contacts, organizations, opportunities, projects, tasks, and notes — directly from Arcade-powered agents and workflows.

## Capabilities

- **Record CRUD** — Create or update contacts, leads, organizations, opportunities, projects, and tasks; retrieve any single record by ID.
- **Lead conversion** — Convert a qualified lead into a contact, organization, and optional opportunity in one operation, with notes carried forward.
- **Search & discovery** — Search across all major record types by name, email, domain, status, pipeline, owner, due date, and more.
- **Activity & context rollup** — Retrieve a record's recent notes, open tasks, and deal/project state in a single call; roll up one user's open deals and tasks together.
- **Pipeline & forecast views** — List pipelines and their ordered stages; summarize opportunities by stage with counts and values for pipeline forecasting.
- **Account metadata** — List users, lead statuses, lead sources, and the authenticated user's identity to support attribution and assignment.

## Secrets

`INSIGHTLY_API_KEY` — Your Insightly REST API key. Obtain it from your Insightly account by navigating to **User Settings → API Key** (visible only to the logged-in user; each user has their own key). The key authenticates all API requests on behalf of that user, so actions are attributed accordingly. See [Insightly API documentation](https://api.insightly.com/v3.1/Help) for details.

`INSIGHTLY_API_URL` — The base URL for your Insightly API endpoint (e.g., `https://api.insightly.com/v3.1`). Insightly may assign different regional or plan-specific endpoints; confirm the correct URL from your Insightly account settings or the API documentation above. This secret lets the toolkit target the right endpoint without hardcoding it.

For configuring secrets in Arcade, see the [Arcade secrets guide](https://docs.arcade.dev/en/guides/create-tools/tool-basics/create-tool-secrets). You can manage secrets at [https://api.arcade.dev/dashboard/auth/secrets](https://api.arcade.dev/dashboard/auth/secrets).
